serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one to two slices of a 30 cm pizza, each slice weighing approximately 80–120 g, yielding roughly 160–245 kcal per slice.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (cr_id G00010740, confidence 0.95). All per_100g values are preserved from the input record as provided by HPB. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central cheese pizza analogue (USDA SR Legacy #21299) as approximately 2.8 g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not reported by HPB; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for truffle wild mushroom pizza. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: cheese pizza on refined wheat dough, GI ~60 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The refined wheat dough base dominates the glycaemic response; wild mushroom and truffle oil toppings do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 32.38) / 100 ≈ 19.4 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15.75 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium itself is moderate at 130 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is relatively low at 130 mg/100g, the high sugar load (15.75 g/100g) with insufficient fibre (1.7 g/100g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and endothelial stress; patients with hypertension should limit portion to one slice and request reduced-sugar dough preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (15.75 g/100g) combined with high refined carbohydrate (32.38 g/100g) and low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g) produces a high glycaemic load and rapid postprandial glucose excursion
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of 32.38 g/100g refined carbohydrate, 15.75 g/100g sugar, and only 1.7 g/100g dietary fibre yields a high glycaemic load (~19 per 100 g); individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit intake to one slice, request a wholemeal or cauliflower crust to increase fibre, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt the postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g from mozzarella) and high sugar (15.75 g/100g) contribute to atherogenic risk; wild mushroom beta-glucans offer a partially offsetting cardioprotective effect
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The estimated saturated fat of approximately 2.8 g/100g from mozzarella, combined with 15.75 g/100g sugar, poses a moderate cardiovascular risk; patients with CVD should limit to one slice per meal, choose a reduced-cheese preparation, and note that wild mushroom beta-glucans may modestly support LDL reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Wild mushroom beta-glucans and truffle oil polyphenols are anti-inflammatory, but high sugar (15.75 g/100g) may promote pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Wild mushrooms provide beta-glucans that modulate immune response and reduce inflammation, and truffle oil contributes polyphenols; however, the high sugar content (15.75 g/100g) may promote inflammatory mediators, so patients with rheumatoid arthritis should request reduced-sugar preparation and limit to one to two slices.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza with Truffle and Wild Mushroom is a Japanese-Western fusion pizza served in Singapore, made from scratch using a fermented, hand-stretched wheat dough base topped with a medley of wild mushrooms (such as shiitake, oyster, and porcini), truffle oil, mozzarella cheese, garlic, and aromatic herbs including thyme and rosemary. The crust is thin to medium in thickness, yielding a chewy, slightly crisp texture with an earthy, umami-rich flavour profile from the wild mushrooms and the distinctive aromatic depth of truffle oil. Per 100 g, the dish provides 204 kcal, 32.38 g carbohydrate (of which 15.75 g sugar), 7.53 g fat, 1.84 g protein, 1.7 g dietary fibre, and 130 mg sodium. The high sugar content relative to fibre raises the glycaemic load to approximately 19 per 100 g (GI ~60, medium), making portion control important for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension. Wild mushroom beta-glucans offer immune-modulating and potentially cardioprotective benefits. The dish is vegetarian but contains gluten and dairy allergens. It is typically served as a lunch or dinner item and is popular in Japanese-style pizzerias and fusion restaurants in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of truffle wild mushroom pizza?
Is wild mushroom pizza good for blood pressure?
What is the GI of mushroom pizza?
What allergens are in truffle mushroom pizza?
How much sugar is in wild mushroom pizza?

Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza analogue and documented. GI estimated from the closest approved analogous food (cheese pizza, University of Sydney GI Database) with full documentation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific truffle wild mushroom pizza preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Saturated fat estimated at 2.8 g/100g from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza (USDA #21299) as the closest approved analogue; this value should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. The input record lists primary_cuisine as Japanese, which reflects the Japanese-style pizzeria context in Singapore rather than traditional Japanese cuisine; the Japanese-language name has been provided accordingly per G6 multilingual rules. Sugar value of 15.75 g/100g is notably high for a savoury pizza and may reflect sauce base composition or dough sugar additions; this value is preserved as reported by HPB at confidence 0.95 but flagged for review.
